course_description.overview: Amateur sportive on closed roads shared with UCI WorldTour Eschborn-Frankfurt pro race, featuring Taunus climbs including Feldberg and Mammolshainer Stich. Three routes: Skyline (40 km flat), Taunus Express (92 km), Taunus Classic (103 km pro-style).

key_segments.name: Großer Feldberg
key_segments.description: Iconic Taunus mountain ascent mirroring pro climbs.

key_segments.name: Mammolshainer Stich
key_segments.description: Legendary steep climb for pro-like challenge.

key_segments.name: Frankfurt Skyline loop
key_segments.description: Relaxed city tour through Eschborn and Frankfurt skyline.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Ranges from beginner-friendly flat city loop to demanding 100+ km with substantial Taunus elevation and steep pitches, matching pro route intensity.

logistics.getting_there: Start at Rathausplatz, Eschborn; accessible by train to Frankfurt/Eschborn; expos in Frankfurt (Apr 30) and Eschborn (May 1).
logistics.accommodation: Frankfurt hotels; event near city center Alte Oper finish.
logistics.registration_details: Open now via Njuko platform; pre-registration recommended for spots; starting blocks assigned by pace, 8:45-9:50 AM start.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bag drop-off at Eschborn site (ADAC bags only); mobile toilets available.
logistics.local_considerations: May 1 Labor Day festival atmosphere; corporate teams welcome; extras like medal engraving, photos, jerseys.

final_verdict.one_liner: Premier German amateur sportive on pro WorldTour roads with Taunus punch.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ambitious amateurs seeking pro experience, beginners on short route, corporate teams.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for closed-road prestige and festival vibe near Frankfurt.
pro_tips: Pre-register early for preferred route and pricing.
Choose start block by honest pace to avoid DQ.
Opt for Taunus Classic to tackle Feldberg and Mammolshainer Stich.
Use bag drop and expo for logistics.
